title: Bridge Essay: To be or not to be: The active inference of suicide.
concept: Predictive processing — free energy principle, Bayesian brain, active inference, neural causality.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Predictive processing — free energy principle, Bayesian brain, active inference, neural causality.

**To be or not to be: The active inference of suicide.** - Neurosci Biobehav Rev (2024). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2023.105531. PMID: 38176631. Suicide presents an apparent paradox as a behavior whose motivation is not obvious since its outcome is non-existence and cannot be experienced. To address this paradox, we propose to frame suicide in the integrated theory of stress and active inference. We present an active inference-based cognitive model of suicide as a type of stress response hanging in cognitive balance between predicting self-preservation and self-destruction. In it, self-efficacy emerges as a meta-cognitive regulator that
